plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate extreme risk.
Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce sector concentration and single-name risk.
Significantly trim SPY to lower overall concentration and build a cash buffer.
Add to QQQI for income generation and stability.
Initiate a new position in VST, a high-quality setup.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a larger cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for lower, steadier returns around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, focusing on capital preservation and building a substantial cash buffer to weather market downturns.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to lower semiconductor concentration.
Trim SPY to address concentration and create room for new positions.
Add to CRWD to increase exposure to a strong AI/Software name.
Initiate new positions in AMD and SNOW, both strong scanner setups.
This plan balances risk reduction with targeted growth in quality tech.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly return with a risk-first approach. It trims leverage and concentration while adding high-quality tech and strong risk/reward setups, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.

plan: Trim SPY and QQQI to reduce broad index and income exposure.
Increase exposure to SMH and CRWD, focusing on strong sectors.
Add to SOXL, increasing leveraged ETF exposure for aggressive growth.
Initiate new positions in AMD and SNOW, leveraging top scanner setups.
This plan concentrates capital in high-conviction tech and semiconductors.
Maintain a small cash buffer, relying on strict stop discipline.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for higher returns around 4% monthly by leaning into leverage and concentration in top-scoring tech and semiconductor names. Expect significantly higher potential drawdowns due to increased leverage and leveraged ETF decay; strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta single names (CRWD, SMH).
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and create space for income assets.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation to maximize monthly distributions.
Initiate a new position in VST, a quality name with potential income.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income over growth.
Focuses on low drawdown and reliable distributions from income-focused assets.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through steady distributions. It eliminates volatile assets, maximizes holdings in income-generating ETFs like QQQI, and maintains a conservative stance to prioritize capital preservation and reliable cash flow.
